text.title: Read-Only Investigation by Default Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments of inc-read-only-investigation-default. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: During an SEV1, agent granted broad write to fix a thing; permission never revoked; next incident agent restarts wrong cluster.
antipattern.root-cause: No time-bounded grant + no post-incident revert.
antipattern.fix: Grants carry expiry; revert script runs at incident close.

antipattern.symptom: Approval token scope `*:*`; agent rolls back the wrong service.
antipattern.root-cause: Token scope not bound to action class.
antipattern.fix: Token scope MUST be `verb:resource`; wildcard rejected.

antipattern.symptom: Incident commander grants `cluster-admin` in panic; agent rolls cascading restarts.
antipattern.root-cause: No two-person approval on permission expansion.
antipattern.fix: Permission expansion requires two-person rule + audit.
